summary.intent: Run a CEO-level review (v3) for CHA-19, validating the engineering review's scope corrections and producing a final plan for the CRM tab Web UI implementation.
summary.outcome: The CEO review v2 was validated — the engineering review's correction to use WebSocket RPC instead of REST was confirmed correct. Two compile-blocking bugs were identified in the current branch, and a final review plan was written to plans/CHA-19-ceo-review-v2.md.

repo: All SPA pages use WebSocket RPC via sendRpc() for data access — zero pages use REST for CRUD. REST endpoints would be pure duplication.
14 CRM RPC methods already exist in gateway/src/methods/services.rs (crm.contacts.list, crm.contacts.upsert, crm.matters.list, etc.) — fully implemented and tested.
Feature gating uses #[cfg(feature = "crm")] in gateway + CLI; web UI should add crm_enabled: bool to GonData matching the graphql_enabled pattern.
The page module pattern is: single large file (page-channels.js is 1342 lines) with internal Preact components — do not split prematurely.
NavCounts uses tokio::join! for concurrent queries; adding CRM count adds ~1ms latency.
SqliteCrmStore.list_filtered does NOT escape % and _ wildcards in search terms — searching for % matches all contacts (low severity, single-tenant).
ON DELETE SET NULL on matters means deleting a contact orphans its matters rather than cascade-deleting them — this is by design.

code.finding: All 14 CRM RPC methods are registered here — crm.contacts.list/get/upsert/delete, crm.channels.list/upsert/delete, crm.matters.list/get/upsert/delete, crm.interactions.list/record/delete
build_nav_counts and GonData needed crm_enabled field + CRM count query; two compile-blocking bugs were found in the branch's implementation of these additions

workflow: The orchestration pipeline runs CEO review → eng review → CEO review v2 as distinct phases, each appending to a Linear workpad comment rather than overwriting.
Headless agent mode requires no interactive commands; all decisions must be resolved autonomously with rationale documented.
Running type-check and tests before any implementation is mandatory per CLAUDE.md; session startup failures must be fixed before new work begins.
Plans are saved to plans/ directory in the branch (e.g., plans/CHA-19-ceo-review-v2.md) for full review history.

friction: Files were not at expected paths initially — required filesystem exploration before reading key files.
CEO review v1 incorrectly recommended ~15 new REST API handlers, requiring a full scope correction in the eng review and re-validation in CEO review v2.
Two compile-blocking bugs were found in the branch's templates.rs changes that were not caught by prior reviews.
open_items: E2E Playwright test implementation (crm.spec.js) — spec defined but requires E2E infra changes; deferred to separate ticket.
Optimistic locking for concurrent CRM updates — last-write-wins is acceptable for v1 single-tenant, tracked as known limitation.
Contact count optimization — build_nav_counts loads all contacts just to count them; add count_contacts() SELECT COUNT(*) method when perf warrants.
Search LIKE wildcard escaping — % and _ in search terms pass through unescaped; P3, cosmetic issue for single-tenant authenticated users.
French and Chinese CRM locale translations — files created with English fallback strings; proper translations are a separate i18n pass.
4 open questions from CEO review v2 still pending human input: stage filter bar vs dropdown, contact detail URL depth, nav position for CRM link, CRM-disabled 404 behavior.
